title: Installing R and the required packages

yaml.title: Installing R and the required packages

markdown.markdown:
Install R from the [Comprehensive R Archive Network
(CRAN)](https://cran.r-project.org/). Choose the binary
distribution for your operating system.

- **Windows / macOS:** download the installer from CRAN.
- **Linux:** use your distribution's package manager. On Debian /
  Ubuntu:
  ```bash
  sudo apt install r-base r-base-dev
  ```

## RStudio (recommended IDE)

[RStudio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/) is a
free IDE that integrates R, Quarto, and a markdown editor. It is the
recommended way to run the tutorials on this site.

## Quarto

Install Quarto from [quarto.org](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/).
The current version (1.4 or later) is required.

## R packages

Install the R packages used by the tutorials with:

```r
install.packages(c(
  "lme4",      # multilevel models
  "lmerTest",  # p-values and profile CIs for lme4
  "lavaan",    # structural equation modelling
  "dplyr",     # data manipulation
  "tibble",    # modern data frames
  "interactions",  # simple-slopes and interaction plots
  "semPlot",   # SEM path diagrams (used in some tutorials)
  "knitr",     # chunk engine for Quarto / R Markdown
  "rmarkdown"  # document rendering
), repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
```

The `make deps` target in the [`Makefile`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/Makefile)
runs the same command.

## Verifying the install

In a fresh R session, run:

```r
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(lavaan)
library(dplyr)
library(interactions)
```

If all six lines return without error, your install is complete.

## Troubleshooting

### `lavaan` fails to load on Linux

`lavaan` depends on system-level BLAS / LAPACK libraries. On
Debian / Ubuntu, install them with:

```bash
sudo apt install libblas-dev liblapack-dev
```

### `interactions` cannot find `sim_slopes`

The `sim_slopes` function lives in the `interactions` package. If
you have a typo or an older version, install the latest:

```r
install.packages("interactions", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
```

### Quarto cannot find R

If `quarto render` fails with *"cannot find R"*, set the
`QUARTO_R` environment variable to the path of your R binary:

```bash
# macOS / Linux
export QUARTO_R=/usr/local/bin/R

# Windows (PowerShell)
$env:QUARTO_R = "C:\Program Files\R\R-4.3.0\bin\R.exe"
```
